mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for new users.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command sets up a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti application from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster, which may lead to data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your current configuration, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current configuration.
> Upgrades the Kagenti application to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 192.0.2.1:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: release kagenti failed: timed out waiting for the condition'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release is stuck and not progressing.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kagenti' to check for issues and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: cannot re-use a name that is still in use'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release name is already in use.

**Fix:**
Use 'helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti' to remove the existing release before reinstalling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: no resources found in kagenti namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kagenti application has not been installed properly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Helm installation command was executed successfully and check the namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed.
